calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Creditor'S Claim
Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anthony Martin appeared by telephone for Eugene Thomas (present). Wendell Baker (Claimant) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-14-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anthony Martin for and with Eugene Thomas. Brian Connors for Key National Funding.

Mr. Martin stated that his Client was disputing Mr. Baker's Creditor's Claim in the amount of $22,000.00 because there was no proof that Mr. Baker made payment for the repairs, that Mr. Baker made the arrangements for the repairs, that Mr. Baker had not produced cancelled checks/receipts and that the three month period on the Creditor's Claim ended yesterday. In addition, Mr. Martin stated that his Client received an e mail stating that Mr. Baker filed an 850 petition but that it was not appearing on the register of actions, that his Client was not disputing that the work was done, that the permit indicated the amount of $5,000.00, that there was the issue as to who made payment and who was entitled to reimbursement, that the amount due to Key National Funding was still in the Attorney Trust Fund, and explained the extent of services provided by Mr. Thomas to support the extraordinary fee request. Mr. Connors stated that Key National Funding was reviewing their contracts and considering removing some of the items (#10.M, #14, and #15) raised in the Examiner's notes from the contracts for the future, and presented oral argument to support his position with regard to Key National Funding's right to petition to the Court to liquidate assets as an Assignee. The Court granted the petition (subject to the order being revised). A reserve amount of $22,000.00 is to be put aside until the Creditor's Claim is resolved and status for the Creditor's Claim was set for June 22, 2010. The Court stated that Mr. Dias' inaction amounted to a waiver of his share of the statutory fees and that the fees were to be divided between Mr. Thompson and Mr. Martin.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5520. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Bloom.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-10-2010 Continued To Apr-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1620 - 1628 Kirkwood Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 90723-2822)
Off Calendar Aug-12-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anthony Martin for and with Eugene Thomas (Petitioner).

The sale was confirmed at $410,000.00. Bond was waived. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Jackson.
